title: Polypodium brevipilum
intro: Polypodium brevipilum, also known as Polypody or Piptoporus, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium brevipilum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Polypodium brevipilum is often processed into a crude herb, decoction, or tea, which is used to treat various health conditions, while in modern herbal medicine, it is also available in capsule or tincture form.
taxonomy: Polypodium brevipilum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Polypodium, which includes over 900 species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various species of ferns. The order Polypodiales is a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the species of ferns and their allies. The class Polypodiopsida is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all the vascular plants that reproduce via spores.
